resume: Experimental physicist specializing in quantum optics and nanophotonics, with a strong track record of rapidly building end-to-end systems. Led a research subgroup from an empty optical table to a Nature Physics publication in 2.5 years. Deep expertise in nanofabrication, tabletop optics, programming and automation, quantum theory, and materials synthesis.
cv: I build precision optical and nanophotonic systems that combine instrumentation, software automation, and quantitative analysis.

My work has focused on molecule-based quantum photonics: designing cryogenic confocal platforms, developing automated control and data pipelines, fabricating nanophotonic devices, and creating materials synthesis workflows for photonic integration.

Across graduate and postdoctoral projects, I have delivered high-impact experimental results by rapidly standing up new systems, iterating through difficult engineering constraints, and translating ambiguous research goals into auditable technical execution.

items: Cryogenic confocal microscopy
Single-molecule spectroscopy (PLE, linewidth, saturation, lifetime)
Photon correlation Fourier spectroscopy (PCFS)
Hanbury Brown-Twiss and time-domain photon counting
Laser and cavity alignment, polarization suppression (>70 dB)
Fiber-coupled and free-space optical systems
Python experiment automation and instrument orchestration
Custom device drivers (TCP/IP, NI-DAQ, serial, hardware APIs)
Long-running unattended acquisition with full audit trails
Data reduction and model-fitting workflows for large datasets
QuTiP and Monte Carlo simulation for collective-emitter modeling
Embedded tooling with ESP32, Arduino, and Raspberry Pi
Nanophotonic cavity and grating coupler design
E-beam lithography, RIE, thin-film process flow
SEM metrology and process iteration
Tidy3D, Lumerical, MEEP/MPB, KLayout
COMSOL electromagnetics
Vapor-phase growth of DBT:anthracene nanosheets
Two-stage tube-furnace protocol development
Micropositioner-based crystal transfer and placement
Nanophotonic integration of organic emitter crystals

resumeSkillLines: Optics - Cryogenic confocal microscopy, fiber optics, laser and cavity alignment
Nanofabrication - e-beam lithography, optical lithography, reactive ion etching
Programming and automation - Python, TCP/IP, ESP32, Arduino, Raspberry Pi

selectedProjects.title: Automated cryogenic quantum optics experiment
selectedProjects.summary: Built a new quantum optics experiment from scratch and delivered a first-author Nature Physics result in two years.
highlights: Designed and constructed a cryogenic confocal microscope with APD, camera, Ti:Saph laser with frequency scanning, beam steering, fast laser switching (800 picoseconds).
Built automated control software to search unattended through quantum emitters over several days for signatures of pairwise interactions, resulting in the observation of superradiance.

link.label: Publication: Lange et al. Nature Physics (2024)

selectedProjects.title: Molecule-nanophotonic integration
selectedProjects.summary: Led an engineering effort to integrate individual molecules with planar nanophotonic resonators.
highlights: Designed and fabricated silicon nitride cavities with $Q = 10{,}000$ and mode volumes of 2.5$(\lambda/n)^3$.
Developed process to microposition crystals doped with quantum emitters on top of planar nanophotonics, achieving nanophotonic-molecule coupling with a cooperativity of $C=0.5$.

link.label: Publication: Lange et al. (ACS Nano, accepted, 2025)

selectedProjects.patentNote: Patent: Photon Emission with Organic Molecules and Nanophotonics. U.S. Provisional Patent Application 63/814,571 (2025), Purdue Research Foundation Ref: 2025-HOOD-71146-01

selectedProjects.title: Extreme aspect ratio crystals doped with quantum emitters
selectedProjects.summary: Developed a protocol to grow extreme aspect ratio crystals doped with quantum emitters and compatible with nanophotonic integration.
highlights: Developed a protocol to grow extreme aspect ratio crystals doped with quantum emitters and compatible with nanophotonic integration.
Aspect ratio: 30 microns wide, 200 nanometers thick; Surface roughness: $<0.8$ nanometers RMS; Doping concentration: $>1000\,/\,\mu\text{m}^3$; Quantum emitter coherence: $T_2/(2T_1)=88\%$.
The protocol is simple, high-yield, highly controllable, and is already being adopted by leading groups worldwide.

link.label: Publication: Keni and Lange et al. (under review, 2026)

researchExperience.role: Postdoctoral researcher | Quantum Photonic Integrated Design Center (QuPIDC)
bullets: Implemented a photon-correlation-Fourier-spectroscopy technique to characterize quantum emitters. Performed the technique on a noisy and unstable emitter (perovskite quantum dots) to demonstrate its robustness (manuscript in preparation)
Led collaboration efforts between researchers in different subfields.
